extern type Number;
extern type Bool;
cap Sub { fn sub(a: Self, b: Self): Self }
extern fn js_sub(a: Number, b: Number): Number;
extern fn js_is_zero(n: Number): Bool;
impl Number: Sub { fn sub(a: Self, b: Self): Self = resume(js_sub(a, b)) }

fn is_even(n: Number): Number = if js_is_zero(n) { 1 } else { is_odd(Sub.sub(n, 1)) }
fn is_odd(n: Number): Number = if js_is_zero(n) { 0 } else { is_even(Sub.sub(n, 1)) }
fn main(): Number = is_even(4)

===EXPECT===
# Mutual recursion: `is_even` calls `is_odd` and vice versa. v2 lifts the v1
# limitation via SCC-level dep-free analysis: Tarjan groups {is_even, is_odd}
# into a single SCC, classifies the whole SCC DepFree, and commits
# perform_resolution for both bodies atomically.
#
# Both fns are DepFree and are cloned (to `is_even__lto` / `is_odd__lto`).
# `main` calls `is_even__lto` directly. `is_even__lto` has `Sub.sub` inlined
# to a direct `js_sub(a, b)` call.
#
# v2 still inherits the v1 "clone doesn't self-redirect" subtlety: the
# recursive call inside `is_even__lto` still targets the CPS `is_odd` rather
# than `is_odd__lto`. The CPS `is_odd` is kept alive for that reason; because
# no call ever reaches `is_odd__lto` (it's only callable from a rewritten
# `is_even__lto` that v1 doesn't rewrite), `is_odd__lto` itself is DCE'd.
# Functionally correct; the cloned `is_odd__lto` body has its `Sub.sub`
# inlined to `js_sub` — we'd just need a "clone→clone" redirect pass to
# preserve it. Tracked as follow-up.
is_even__lto
js_sub(a, b)
__main_cps
is_even__lto(__caps, 4, __k)
function is_odd(__caps, n, __k)
!function is_even(__caps
